message:
test(mvcc): microbench branching vs branchless visible() predicate

Adds a \`#[ignore]\`d microbench \`bench_visible_branchless\` under
\`mvcc::invariants::tests\` that measures the pure visibility-check
cost of the two shapes of the \`visible()\` predicate — the old
\`commit_seq != 0 && commit_seq <= snapshot.high\` form and the
current branchless \`commit_seq.wrapping_sub(1) < snapshot.high\`
form — so future optimizations to this core MVCC read-path
predicate can be justified with numbers instead of just argued
from the instruction sequence.

Harness design:

- Runs only under \`cargo test -- --ignored\` (the default \`cargo
  test\` lane never sees it), so it cannot add wall-clock or CPU
  pressure to regular CI.
- Synthesizes SAMPLES=4096 page versions with a realistic 80/10/10
  mix (committed-and-visible / uncommitted / newer-than-snapshot)
  so both branches get exercised and the branching form pays its
  worst-case branch-predictor miss cost.
- Defines two \`#[inline(always)]\` local functions
  (\`visible_branching\`, \`visible_branchless\`) that mirror the
  old and current predicates exactly — importantly, the harness
  does NOT call \`invariants::visible\` itself, so renaming or
  refactoring that helper will not silently change which form is
  being measured.
- Runs one warm-up pass per form, then TRIALS=9 paired trials with
  ITERS_PER_TRIAL=2 000 000, wraps the predicate result in
  \`std::hint::black_box\` to defeat dead-store elimination, and
  prints per-trial timings so a human can spot noise.
- Computes the median of each nine-sample set and prints a single
  summary line with both medians and the \`delta%\` of branchless
  vs branching, so the result is greppable from test output.

Test-only addition. No production code touched and no impact on
normal test runtime.
